article.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL

harvestedTerms.summary: A technical deep-dive from ServiceNow AI on migrating vLLM from V0 to V1 for online reinforcement learning (PipelineRL/GSPO), documenting four backend correctness issues — logprob semantics, runtime defaults, inflight weight updates, and fp32 lm_head precision — that caused train-inference mismatch. Highly specialized ML engineering content focused on the numerical correctness surface of RL training pipelines.

mustReadFlagged.reason: A rare piece of honest RL engineering archaeology: the authors document exactly which four backend correctness issues broke their training run during a vLLM migration, and why they fixed infrastructure before touching the RL objective. Useful for anyone building or debugging online RL pipelines with shared inference engines.

article.title: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.

harvestedTerms.summary: Google announces a $3.5 million film competition (Future Vision XPRIZE) in partnership with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners, inviting filmmakers to submit short films envisioning an optimistic AI-forward future, with Google providing production support via its Google Flow AI tool and 100 ZEROS initiative.

article.title: Last Week in AI #340 - OpenAI vs Musk + Microsoft, DeepSeek v4, Vision Banana

harvestedTerms.summary: Weekly AI roundup covering the Musk v. Altman trial (including xAI's distillation admission), OpenAI-Microsoft partnership renegotiation, DeepSeek V4 launch, and Google DeepMind's Vision Banana unified model. Rich in industry/legal drama and a few technically notable releases.

article.title: Parloa builds service agents customers want to talk to

harvestedTerms.summary: Parloa, a Berlin-based startup, uses OpenAI models to build an AI Agent Management Platform (AMP) for enterprise voice-driven customer service. The article details their evaluation-first approach, modular sub-agent architecture, voice pipeline optimization, and LLM-as-a-judge scoring methodology.

article.title: An Interview with Joanna Stern About Living With AI

harvestedTerms.summary: A paywalled Stratechery interview with Joanna Stern about her book on living with AI and her new media company. The excerpt is essentially a subscription gate with no substantive article content visible — only title, metadata, and related links are accessible.

article.title: Using Claude Code: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of HTML

harvestedTerms.summary: Simon Willison links to and reflects on a piece by Thariq Shihipar (Claude Code team at Anthropic) advocating for HTML as a richer output format over Markdown when prompting Claude, with examples of interactive, annotated HTML artifacts generated from prompts. Willison also tries the approach with GPT-5.5 on a Linux exploit explanation.
